summary.intent: Implement task #267: the framework-agnostic TreeView core component in ars-ui, per spec/components/navigation/tree-view.md, including WAI-ARIA keyboard navigation, expand/collapse, selection, typeahead, and the DnD reorder variant (agnostic layer only).
summary.outcome: TreeView core fully implemented (~1700 lines), with 117 unit tests, 26 snapshots, spec-conformance, proptest, and clippy-clean; ars-collections gained all_keys()/all_nodes(); spec tree-view.md extensively rewritten; PR #695 opened; 3 complete Codex review rounds addressed (33 total findings fixed with TDD regressions + spec sync), round 4 committed but replies/re-trigger still pending at session end.

repo: TreeCollection::nodes() iterates visible_indices only (visible-only), not all nodes — ExpandAll/typeahead must use added all_keys()/all_nodes() to reach collapsed subtrees.
Key does not implement Borrow<str>, so spec's String-based .contains(id) / BTreeSet<Key> is not compilable — all node identity must use Key throughout.
EffectName has a blanket impl; implementing it manually conflicts — never add manual EffectName impls.

selection::State::select already rejects disabled keys, but disabled_behavior must be set to DisabledBehavior::FocusOnly (not Skip) so typeahead can still reach disabled nodes while keeping them non-selectable.

typeahead::State (ars_collections) is the canonical shared implementation used by select/listbox/menu/context_menu — accepts (char, now_ms), uses collection text_value, locale-aware when i18n feature enabled.
cargo xtask spec compile-snippets uses syn::parse_file (syntax check only, no type resolution) — spec blocks only need to parse, not type-check against each other.
Navigation tests live in a single-file tests/spec_conformance/navigation.rs; new components append there rather than creating per-component files.
.agents/ and .claude/ skill files are hardlinked to the same inode — editing either updates both.
Pre-commit hook re-stages all tracked modifications, collapsing intended two-commit splits into one — plan for this when separating feature from policy commits.

DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection trait impls require bundling collection + live selection state + DOM hit-testing; the bare collection cannot own selection state, so these belong exclusively in adapter tasks (#366/#509).
Codex review converges over multiple rounds (10→9→7→7 findings across 4 rounds); each round requires TDD fixes + spec sync + clippy + xci-fast + push before re-triggering @codex review.
snapshot-reviewed label must be re-applied on every commit that touches .snap files, not just the initial PR commit.

code.finding: The no-bound impl<T> block is the correct home for pure-read accessors like all_keys()/all_nodes() — the CollectionItem-bound impl block is for typed operations.

selection::State::select already rejects disabled keys, but the disabled_keys set must be explicitly seeded from TreeItem.disabled in init — and disabled_behavior must be FocusOnly, not Skip, to preserve typeahead reachability.

Active-descendant pattern (root tabindex=0 + aria-activedescendant) requires stable node DOM ids; effective_selection_mode must be derived from both props.selection_mode and the collection's allow_multiple_selection flag, and used consistently in init, SyncProps, and aria-multiselectable.

process_typeahead must use reconciled_items(ctx) — cloning ctx.items and applying ctx.expanded via set_expanded — because TreeCollection's internal expansion snapshot is stale relative to live ctx.expanded state.
Accordion's Api requires a manual Debug impl (not derivable due to function pointer fields) — pattern to follow for all complex Api structs.

workflow: zsh associative array iteration (${!arr[@]}) fails in Bash tool; use bash <<'SCRIPT' heredoc to run bash-specific syntax reliably.
cargo-mutants buffers progress to mutants.out/ incrementally; polling stdout directly is unreliable — check mutants.out/missed.txt for final results.
Background pollers can contend on the cargo lock with the main mutation run, causing premature exit with code 1 — distinguish stale poller failures from actual run completion.

The mandatory waiting-for-codex-review loop: post @codex review → poll for 👀 drop / 👍 / new threads → fetch unresolved threads → TDD fix → spec sync → clippy → xci-fast → commit/push → reply inline (gh api) → resolve (resolveReviewThread GraphQL) → re-trigger. Never skip spec sync before pushing.
When adopting a shared multi-arg event type (TypeaheadSearch(char, u64)), all test sites using the old arity break at once — locate all sites before compiling to avoid iterative single-fix cycles.

Removing a mandatory pre-PR gate mid-session (mutation) requires stopping background runs, updating AGENTS.md, and updating the post-implementation-audit skill — all three locations must be kept in sync.

friction: Spec used String for node identity throughout but codebase convention and BTreeSet<Key> correctness required Key — extensive spec rewrite needed across §1.2–§1.6, §3, §4, §6.
TypeaheadSearch(char) had to become TypeaheadSearch(char, u64) when adopting canonical typeahead::State, breaking ~8 test sites and the proptest arbitrary simultaneously.
Two-commit split (feature vs. policy change) collapsed into one because the pre-commit hook re-staged all tracked modifications — the split had to be abandoned and the commit message amended.
zsh ${!arr[@]} associative array syntax failed in the Bash tool when posting bulk Codex thread replies; required rewriting as bash <<'SCRIPT'.

182-mutant background run was stopped mid-flight when the policy changed; the distinction between 'stale poller failed' and 'actual run completed' notifications caused confusion across multiple context boundaries.
Context compaction occurred mid-session (round 4 Codex fixes), requiring a full transcript summary to resume — several tool reads had to be re-done to ground spec edits.
Multiple stale ScheduleWakeup fallback notifications fired after the mutation gate was already cancelled, creating noise and requiring explicit dismissal.
cargo-mutants doesn't stream results to stdout — polling required reading mutants.out/missed.txt rather than task output, which wasn't discovered until after the run completed.

Codex review required 4 rounds (33 total findings) with full fix+sync+push cycles each round — each round took significant wall time due to xci-fast gate (~10 gates including spec compile-snippets on 2171 blocks).
open_items: Codex review round 4: commit pushed but replies/resolution of 7 threads and @codex review re-trigger still pending — must complete before issue #267 can be closed.
Issue #267 must not be closed until PR #695 is merged, CI is green, and Codex gives 👍.

Adapter tasks #366 (TreeView Leptos adapter) and #509 (TreeView Dioxus adapter) still open — DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection trait impls, pointer hit-testing, hover-expand timer, and use_drag/use_drop wiring deferred to those tasks.

6 missed mutants from the original mutation run had killer tests added (all 8 including 2 timeouts), but the rerun was stopped before confirming 0 missed — Nightly CI will now be the source of truth per updated policy.
Lazy loading (§5) and Renamable Nodes (§6) are explicitly gated out-of-scope variants in tree-view.md — not implemented, tracked as future work.

reconciled_items() clones the entire TreeCollection and applies ctx.expanded one key at a time via single-key set_expanded — a bulk set_expansion_map method on TreeCollection would be more efficient and worth adding as a follow-up.
